key_insight: A DRX-aware physics-informed neural network can model hot deformation in a Mo-rich Ti alloy while preserving constitutive physics and recrystallization behavior.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable process design and simulation for thermomechanical forming of titanium alloys across wide temperature and strain-rate ranges.
method: Trained stacked residual physics-informed neural networks on experimental flow-stress curves from 800–1050 °C and 0.01–10 s⁻¹.
Embedded constitutive constraints such as strain hardening, thermal softening, strain-rate sensitivity, and post-peak softening via automatic differentiation.
Added a dual-output DRX-aware architecture with JMAK-Avrami regularization, DRX saturation, and Arrhenius consistency to link recrystallization fraction to stress.
evidence_signal: The DRX-aware model reported RMSE = 11.69 MPa, MAE = 4.83 MPa, R² = 0.9850, and cross-validated RMSE = 12.47 ± 0.26 MPa, while reproducing flow curves, DRX kinetics, and Zener–Hollomon trends.
watch_item: The approach depends on careful tuning of physics-loss terms and may be sensitive when extrapolating beyond the tested temperature and strain-rate envelope.
paper_title: Physics-Informed Residual Deep Learning for Constitutive Modeling of Hot Deformation and Dynamic Recrystallization in a Mo-Rich $α+β$ Titanium Alloy
